commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: No local insurance coverage for GLP-1. Cash price at Farmacia Arrocha or imported.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Available at local pharmacies, no prescription required for many medications

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Farmacia Arrocha (major chain), Metro Plus, and independent farmacias. Many medications available without prescription. Zona Libre in Colón offers discounted medications.
medicine.notes: Panama has no universal drug coverage for expats. CSS (Caja de Seguro Social) covers residents who contribute. Most expats pay cash. Pensionado visa holders get 15-25% pharmacy discounts. GLP-1 drugs are expensive as they must be imported.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Panama at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: +Movil Postpaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~$10/line. +Movil best coverage. ITBMS tax included.

items.name: Ground Beef / Turkey
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Fish (local catch)
items.notes: Fresh local fish often cheaper than US

items.name: Coffee (ground/beans)
items.notes: Excellent local coffee in most LATAM countries

items.name: Premium Dog Food (kibble)
items.notes: Imported premium brands cost more; local brands available

breakdown.notes: No rental tax in Panama. Condo fees (cuota de mantenimiento) common in apartment buildings.

housing.taxNotes: No rental income tax for tenants. No VAT/ITBMS on residential rent. Property tax exemptions for new construction (up to 20 years).
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month lease standard. Security deposit 1 month rent. Pensionado visa holders may get preferential lease terms.
housing.marketNotes: Panama City most expensive. Boquete and beach towns significantly cheaper. USD currency eliminates exchange risk.

stores.name: Riba Smith
stores.notes: Premium supermarket, excellent imported selection, expat favorite

stores.name: Super 99
stores.notes: Largest local chain, good prices on local products

stores.name: PriceSmart
stores.notes: Costco-style warehouse club, great for bulk buying

healthcare.coverageSummary: CSS public system available to Pensionado visa holders. Private insurance $200-350/mo per person. Many expats use private hospitals exclusively.

gpVisit.notes: Private GP visits $30-50. Pensionado discount 15-25% at many providers.

specialist.notes: Specialists $60-100 without insurance. Hospital Punta Pacifica (Johns Hopkins affiliated).

privateInsurance.notes: ASSA, Mapfre, or international plans. Pensionado visa may offer group rates.

emergency.notes: Private ER visit $300-800. CSS emergency care available but lower quality.

dental.notes: Good quality dental care. Prices 40-60% less than US.

healthcare.notes: Panama has Johns Hopkins-affiliated hospital. Pensionado visa holders get 15-25% discounts on healthcare. Panama City has best facilities.

gymMonthly.notes: PowerClub, Gold's Gym available in Bocas del Toro, Panama

discounts.name: Jubilado/Pensionado Discounts
discounts.description: One of the world's best senior discount programs. Women 55+, men 60+ (or retirees of any age with Pensionado visa).

discounts.name: Medical Services
discounts.description: 15-25% off medical consultations, dental, eye exams, and hospital services.

discounts.name: Transportation
discounts.description: 25% off domestic flights, 15% off bus fares, 15-20% off boat/ferry travel.

discounts.name: Restaurants & Entertainment
discounts.description: 10-25% off restaurant meals (Mon-Thu typically), 25-50% off movies, entertainment, and hotels.

discounts.name: Utilities & Services
discounts.description: 25% off electricity (first 500 kWh), 25% off water, 25% off phone service. One of the most generous utility discount programs globally.

discounts.name: Property Tax Exemption
discounts.description: Tax exemption on primary residence (up to $120,000 assessed value). New construction exempt up to 20 years.

discounts.name: Closing Costs
discounts.description: 15% off mortgage closing costs and professional services (lawyers, architects).

monthlyPass.notes: RapiPass rechargeable card for Metro and MetroBus. Expanding system.

publicTransit.coverage: Metro lines and MetroBus in Panama City. Bus networks in other cities.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Pensionado/jubilado RapiPass for free metro and bus rides.

rideShare.availability: Uber and DiDi available in Panama City. Yellow taxis use zones. InDriver for negotiated fares.
rideShare.notes: Ride-share widely used in Bocas Del Toro. Negotiate taxi fares before riding if no meter.

registration.notes: Vehicle registration through local transit authority in Bocas Del Toro.

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. International driving permit accepted for first 90 days.

fuel.notes: Fuel prices in USD. Government-subsidized in some cases.

parking.notes: Street parking available in Bocas Del Toro. Malls and supermarkets usually have free parking.

tolls.notes: Toll roads on major highways between cities.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Pensionado 15% discount on auto insurance premiums at some providers.

transportation.walkability: Moderate. Walkable in some urban areas. Hot climate limits walking distance.

cycling.infrastructure: Bicycle infrastructure varies in Bocas Del Toro. Urban areas gradually improving cycling lanes.
cycling.notes: Heat and terrain may limit cycling as primary transport.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Growing EV market in Panama City. BYD, Hyundai, Kia dealers. Law 295 incentives. Import costs may apply.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Law 295 (2022): EVs exempt from import duties and ITBMS (7% sales tax). Reduced annual vehicle tax.

chargingHome.notes: Home charging feasible in houses and some apartments in Bocas Del Toro.

chargingPublic.notes: Public charging network growing in Panama. Check availability in Bocas Del Toro.

insurance.notes: EV insurance through major local providers.

maintenance.notes: EV service availability growing. Authorized dealers in major cities.

registration.notes: Reduced or exempt registration for EVs.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard warranty. Service access best in major cities.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV resale market developing in Panama. Larger cities have better resale prospects.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging, insurance, maintenance (monthly avg), registration. Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.
